perturbation.original_text: 1.18 "Test(s)" shall mean the Assay, which is a laboratory developed test, and/or any Collaboration Assay which is added to this Agreement pursuant to Section 3.5(e), performed as a clinical reference laboratory test.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.18 "Test(s)" shall mean the Assay, which is a laboratory developed test, and/or any Collaboration Assay which is added to this Agreement pursuant to Section 3.5(e), performed as a clinical reference laboratory test, only if it is explicitly approved by both Biocept and Life Technologies in writing before the test being executed.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Test(s)' as the Assay or any Collaboration Assay added according to Section 3.5(e). The modified text introduces a condition of explicit written approval from both parties before the test can be considered a 'Test(s)' under the agreement. This contradicts Section 3.5(e), which outlines the process for adding Collaboration Assays and implies that once the procedure in 3.5(e) is followed, it automatically qualifies as a 'Test(s)' without needing additional explicit written approval, causing an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Collaboration Assays. During the Term, Biocept shall keep Life Technologies reasonably apprised of its plans to add analytes to the Assay. In addition, Life Technologies may desire for Biocept to develop a specific new analytes for the Assay (for example, the inclusion of additional mutations to the mutation analysis component of the Assay), to be offered by the parties as an additional Test under this Agreement. In either case, the parties shall negotiate in good faith an amendment to this Agreement that will govern the development (as needed) and commercialization of such Tests with new analytes (each a "Collaboration Assay"), which amendment may include financial support, contributions of and access to each party's technology and/or clinical samples, milestones, timing of the development effort, exclusivity and ownership rights. Any such agreed upon Collaboration Assay development shall be performed by Biocept or jointly as the parties may agree. Once the parties have agreed upon a plan relating to the development of a particular Collaboration Assay, if development is needed (each, a "Project"), the parties shall reduce such agreement to writing, which shall include a project plan which will set forth each party's obligations with respect to the Project (each, a "Project Plan") and thereafter, such Collaboration Assay shall be deemed a Test for all purposes under this Agreement and shall be subject to the terms of this Agreement as amended. Each such Project Plan shall be attached as a part of Exhibit D to this Agreement following written acceptance thereof by both parties without any additional action required on the part of either party. Any amendments or revisions to a Project Plan shall be mutually agreed upon by the parties in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Test(s)' included Collaboration Assays added pursuant to Section 3.5(e). The changed text adds a requirement for explicit written approval from both parties for any test to be considered a 'Test(s).' This directly contradicts Section 3.5(e) which states 'thereafter, such Collaboration Assay shall be deemed a Test for all purposes under this Agreement' after the Project Plan is accepted. The changed text introduces a new requirement that is not present in, and is logically inconsistent with, the procedure outlined in 3.5(e), thereby creating an in-text contradiction as defined in the Structural Flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: 9.4 Disclaimer. Except as expressly set forth herein, THE TECHNOLOGY, MATERIALS AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS PROVIDED BY EACH PARTY HEREUNDER ARE PROVIDED "AS IS," AND EACH PARTY EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ANY AND ALL WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING THE WARRANTIES OF DESIGN, MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, NON- INFRINGEMENT OF THE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS OF THIRD PARTIES, OR ARISING FROM A COURSE OF DEALING, USAGE OR TRADE PRACTICES.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.4 Disclaimer. Except as expressly set forth herein, THE TECHNOLOGY, MATERIALS AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS PROVIDED BY EACH PARTY HEREUNDER ARE PROVIDED "AS IS," AND EACH PARTY EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ANY AND ALL WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING THE WARRANTIES OF DESIGN, MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, NON- INFRINGEMENT OF THE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS OF THIRD PARTIES, OR ARISING FROM A COURSE OF DEALING, USAGE OR TRADE PRACTICES. Biocept specifically warrants that the Assay will be free from defects in design and materials.
perturbation.explanation: The original text is a standard 'as is' disclaimer, where both parties disclaim all warranties. The added sentence, 'Biocept specifically warrants that the Assay will be free from defects in design and materials,' directly contradicts the 'as is' disclaimer by creating a specific express warranty regarding the Assay. This undermines the general disclaimer and creates uncertainty about the level of assurance provided for the Assay. The specific warrant creates in-text contradiction with the original intent of the disclaimer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.4 Disclaimer. Except as expressly set forth herein, THE TECHNOLOGY, MATERIALS AND INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS PROVIDED BY EACH PARTY HEREUNDER ARE PROVIDED "AS IS," AND EACH PARTY EXPRESSLY DISCLAIMS ANY AND ALL WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING THE WARRANTIES OF DESIGN, MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, NON- INFRINGEMENT OF THE INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS OF THIRD PARTIES, OR ARISING FROM A COURSE OF DEALING, USAGE OR TRADE PRACTICES. Biocept specifically warrants that the Assay will be free from defects in design and materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly disclaims all warranties, providing the technology 'as is'. The changed text introduces a specific warranty for the Assay regarding defects in design and materials. This directly contradicts the 'as is' disclaimer in the same section, creating an in-text contradiction. The contradicted text and the changed text are in the same location, but that doesn't mean there isn't a contradiction, the changed text introduces a warranty to a text disclaiming all warranties, therefore, contradiction exists.
